name: Opt out of overdraft service (Reg E)
summary: One-click action generating the request to revoke opt-in consent for overdraft service on ATM and one-time debit card transactions under Regulation E, eliminating future overdraft fees on those transaction types.

evidence.claim: Regulation E (12 CFR §1005.17) requires banks to obtain affirmative opt-in consent before charging overdraft fees on ATM withdrawals and one-time debit card transactions. Consumers may revoke this opt-in at any time and banks must comply as soon as reasonably practicable.
CFPB 2023 guidance: revocation of overdraft opt-in is effective immediately upon receipt by the bank; banks may not impose unreasonable delays.
CFPB Model Form A-9 — standard Reg E opt-in/opt-out notice template banks use; reversing consent follows the same channel.

body.description: When Lens detects the user has been charged an overdraft fee or identifies that the user's account has opt-in overdraft service enabled, it offers a one-click action to draft the revocation message, direct the user to the bank's in-app opt-out toggle (if known), and provide the CFPB complaint path if the bank resists.

prerequisites.description: Overdraft fee line item identified on statement, OR user has explicitly opted in to overdraft per Reg E.
User confirms they want to opt out of overdraft on ATM/one-time debit transactions (understanding that transactions will decline instead).

template.draftSubject: Revocation of overdraft opt-in under Regulation E (12 CFR §1005.17)
recommendedChannels: Bank mobile app: Settings → Account Services → Overdraft Service → Opt Out
Phone: call the number on the back of the debit card
Written: send the above letter by secure message in online banking (creates audit trail)

bankKnownPaths.chase: Chase Mobile → Profile & Settings → Account → Debit Card Coverage → Opt Out
bankKnownPaths.bank-of-america: BofA App → Menu → Help & Support → Set Account Services → Change Overdraft Setting
bankKnownPaths.wells-fargo: Wells Fargo Mobile → Menu → Account Services → Manage Overdraft Services
bankKnownPaths.capital-one: Already opted out bank-wide as of 2022 (no fees charged); verify in app if account pre-dates.

successSignals.description: Bank confirms opt-out by email, secure message, or account-settings display.
No overdraft fees appear on statement in subsequent billing cycles.
